summary:
This Distributor Agreement, effective September 7, 1999, appoints Electric City of Illinois LLC as the exclusive distributor of energy-efficiency devices ("Products") for Electric City Corp. within the Illinois market. The contract spans a ten-year term, renewable for an additional decade.

The Company is obligated to supply requested Products, provide promotional materials, and offer technical support. The Distributor must exclusively purchase from the Company, fund marketing efforts, and meet escalating annual minimum unit purchase quotas, backed by a $500,000 irrevocable letter of credit.

Risks include the potential loss of exclusive distribution rights if the Distributor fails to meet purchase quotas, unless performance is hindered by force majeure. The agreement mandates mutual indemnification for breaches or negligence. Additionally, both parties are bound by strict non-disclosure, non-solicitation, and non-compete covenants that extend beyond the contract’s termination. The Company provides a 24-month product warranty.
termination_clause: 4.2 Termination for Cause. In the event either party (a) becomes adjudicated insolvent, (b) discontinues its business, (c) has voluntary of involuntary bankruptcy proceedings instituted against it, or (d) makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, the other party shall be entitled to terminate this Agreement effective immediately upon written notice.

The Distributor shall not disclose this agreement except upon consent of Company.

summary:
This Promotion and Distribution Agreement, effective August 1, 2011, authorizes Whitesmoke Inc. to bundle and distribute Google Chrome and Google Toolbar with its software. The agreement defines usage criteria, installation requirements, and strict branding guidelines for the "Distributor Apps."

Key obligations include the distributor’s duty to ensure informed end-user consent through mandated EULAs, provide accurate installation reports, and maintain compliance with technical "Criteria Checkers." Google retains rights to audit records and unilaterally adjust distribution guidelines.

Significant risks involve strict termination clauses for material breaches, including unauthorized software modification, failure to adhere to anti-bribery laws, or changes in company control. Both parties are subject to liability limitations, though the contract mandates reciprocal indemnification for third-party intellectual property claims. The agreement is governed by English law, and failure to meet distribution quality standards may result in immediate license revocation.

summary:
This document is a framework supply agreement between Shenzhen LOHAS Supply Chain Management Co., Ltd. (the buyer) and a seller, governing product procurement through designated agents. It outlines terms for future purchase orders, including product specifications, shipping, and payment via Letter of Credit or telegraphic transfer.

Key obligations require the seller to confirm orders within three working days, maintain specific packaging standards, insure goods for 110% of value, and guarantee quality compliance. The buyer’s agents are responsible for issuing orders and managing payments.

Significant risks include strict liabilities for the seller regarding improper packing, shipping delays, or non-conforming goods. Late deliveries incur penalties of 0.5% of the total value per week (capped at 5%). If delivery is delayed over ten weeks, the buyer may cancel the contract and demand a 30% penalty. Disputes are subject to binding arbitration in Beijing under Chinese law.
termination_clause: In case the accident lasts for more than 10 weeks, the Buyers shall have the right to cancel the Contract. In case the Sellers fail to make delivery ten weeks later than the time of shipment stipulated in the Contract, the Buyers have the right to cancel the contract and the Sellers, in spite of the cancellation, shall still pay the aforesaid penalty to the Buyers without delay, the seller should refund the money received and pay the 30% of the total goods price of the penalty.
confidentiality_clause: This final price is the confidential information. Dissemination, distribution or duplication of this price is strictly prohibited.
liability_clause: The seller shall be liable for any damage and loss of the commodity, expenses incurred on account of improper packing, and any damage attributable to inadequate or improper protective measures taken by the seller in regard to the packing. The Sellers shall not be held responsible for the delay in shipment or non-delivery, of the goods due to Force Majeure, which might occur during the process of manufacturing or in the course of loading or transit.

summary:
This Web Site Hosting Agreement between Centrack International and i-on interactive outlines the terms for hosting the Customer’s website. i-on agrees to provide server space, internet connectivity, weekly backups, and limited monthly administrative support. The Customer is responsible for all website content, maintenance, marketing, user support, and security of administrative accounts. The Customer must pay a recurring $450 monthly fee; failure to pay results in service discontinuation. 

Key risks include a strict limitation of liability for i-on, capping damages at one month’s fees, and the Customer’s obligation to indemnify i-on against all third-party claims or legal expenses arising from the Customer's use of services. Furthermore, i-on is held harmless for data loss or service interruptions beyond its explicit control, and the Customer bears full responsibility for the legality and stability of their own installed software components.
termination_clause: The term of this Agreement for the Hosted Site shall commence upon April 1, 1999 and shall continue for a period of six (6) months, unless earlier terminated in accordance with provisions hereof. This Agreement shall automatically be renewed for one (1) or more one (1) month periods unless either the Customer or i-on gives notice to the other party of its intention not to renew the Agreement, which notice must be given not less than fifteen (15) days before the end of the respective initial or renewal term. Either party may terminate this Agreement without cause at any time effective upon thirty (30) days' written notice. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in this Agreement, no termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever shall relieve the Customer of the obligation to pay all amounts due to i-on and to make such payments on a timely basis.
confidentiality_clause: Not found in this contract
liability_clause: i-on will not be liable under any circumstances for any lost profits or other consequential damages, even if i-on has been advised as to the possibility of such damages. i-on's liability for damages to the Customer for any cause whatsoever, regardless of the form of action, and whether in contract or in tort, including negligence, shall be limited to one (1) month's fees and the remaining portion of any prepaid fees. The Customer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless i-on, against any lawsuits, claims, damages, or liabilities (or actions or proceedings in respect thereof) to which i-on may become subject related to or arising out of Customer's use of i-on's services, and will reimburse i-on for all legal and other expenses, including attorney's fees, incurred in connection with investigating, defending, or settling any such loss, claim, damage, liability, action, or proceeding whether or not in connection with pending or threatened litigation in which i-on is a party.

summary: This Joint Filing Agreement is a legal document executed by Shelby J. Butterfield and the Butterfield Family Trust. Its primary purpose is to facilitate the collective filing of Schedule 13D or 13G disclosures with regulatory authorities, as required under Rule 13d-1(k) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, regarding their shared ownership of issuer securities. Under the agreement, both parties are obligated to ensure the timely submission of these filings and any subsequent amendments. Regarding liability, each party remains strictly responsible for the accuracy and completeness of the information they provide. While they are not held accountable for the data provided by the other party, this liability protection is limited; a party remains responsible if they possess knowledge or have reason to believe that the other’s disclosed information is inaccurate. This structure minimizes collective risk while mandating individual accountability for regulatory compliance.
termination_clause: Not found in this contract
confidentiality_clause: Not found in this contract
liability_clause: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments, and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning her or it contained therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the other, except to the extent that she or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.

summary:
This endorsement agreement, effective January 13, 2005, establishes a professional relationship between golfer Tom Watson ("Consultant") and Adams Golf, Ltd. The purpose is for Adams Golf to secure exclusive rights to use the Consultant’s name, likeness, and endorsement to market and promote their golf products globally.

The Consultant is obligated to exclusively use and endorse Adams Golf products during professional events, cooperate with design teams, and participate in promotional activities. Adams Golf must supply necessary equipment and provide defined base compensation and performance-based bonuses.

Risks and penalties include mandatory repayment of compensation if the Consultant fails to participate in a minimum number of professional tournaments or if the agreement is terminated prematurely due to breach or inability to compete. Furthermore, Adams Golf may terminate the contract if the Consultant engages in public conduct that causes disrepute or material damage to the company’s brand.

summary:
This Consulting Agreement between Kiromic, Inc. and Gianluca Rotino establishes an independent contractor relationship for business development, marketing, and statistical analysis services. The Consultant is tasked with managing internal consulting duties, supporting product pipeline priorities, and identifying new business opportunities.

Key obligations include maintaining strict confidentiality regarding proprietary information and irrevocably assigning all developed intellectual property (Inventions) to the Company. The Consultant must avoid conflicts of interest, warrant against any prior debarment from healthcare programs, and secure authorization to work in the United States.

Risks and liabilities include a broad indemnification clause requiring the Consultant to cover damages and legal fees arising from the misuse of third-party proprietary information. Furthermore, the Consultant faces potential financial risk regarding tax liabilities, as the Company assumes no responsibility for tax advice or mitigation. Disputes are subject to binding, final arbitration in Houston, Texas.
termination_clause:
Either Consultant or Company may terminate this Agreement upon prior written notice thereof to the other party.

Upon termination of this Agreement, all rights and duties of the parties hereunder shall cease except:

Company shall be obliged to pay, within thirty (30) days after receipt of Consultant's final statement, all amounts owing to Consultant for unpaid Services completed by Consultant and related expenses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of Section 1 hereof, and Sections 2, 3, S(c), 6, 7, 8 and 10 shall survive termination of this Agreement.
confidentiality_clause:
"Confidential Information" means any proprietary information technical data, trade secrets or know-how, including, but not limited to, research and product plans, products, services, markets, developments, inventions, processes, formulas, technology, marketing, finances or other business information disclosed to Consultant by Company either directly or indirectly in writing, orally or otherwise. Confidential Information also includes all Inventions (as defined below) and any other information or materials generated in connection with the Services.

Consultant shall not, during or subsequent to the term of this Agreement, use any Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever other than the performance of the Services on behalf of Company, or disclose Confidential Information to any third party. Consultant agrees that Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of Company. Consultant further agrees to take all reasonable precautions to prevent any unauthorized disclosure or use of Confidential Information. Notwithstanding the above, Consultant's obligation under this Section 2(b) relating to Confidential Information shall not apply to information which (i) is known to Consultant at the time of disclosure to Consultant by Company as evidenced by written records of Consultant, (ii) has been publicly known and made generally available through no wrongful act of Consultant, or (iii) has been rightfully received by Consultant from a third party authorized to make such disclosure.
liability_clause: Consultant will indemnify Company and hold it harmless from and against all claims, liabilities, damages and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees and costs of suit, arising out of or in connection with any violation or claimed violation by Company of such third party's rights resulting in whole or in part from Company's use of the work product of Consultant under this Agreement.

summary:
This agreement serves as an amendment and formal termination of a 2016 Joint Venture Agreement between Veoneer parties and Nissin parties. The primary purpose is to facilitate the divestment of Veoneer’s equity interests in VNBJ and VNBZ to Honda and Nissin, effectively ending the joint venture relationship between these specific entities.

Under this agreement, Veoneer parties are obligated to complete the share purchase transactions. Upon completion, the original Joint Venture Agreement ceases to apply to the Veoneer parties and the respective business entities. Regarding risks and liabilities, the agreement clarifies that termination does not absolve parties from liability for breaches of representations, warranties, or covenants occurring prior to the closing date. Furthermore, the Nissin parties must ensure that VNBJ and VNBZ honor prior indemnification obligations for directors and officers regarding liabilities arising before the transaction closing.
termination_clause:
a.As of the VNBJ Closing, except as expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBJ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBJ under the JV Agreement shall terminate and all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBZ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires.

b.As of the VNBZ Closing, except as expressly set forth in this Amendment, the JV Agreement shall immediately cease to have any application or effect with respect to VNBZ, all rights and obligations with respect to VNBZ under the JV Agreement shall terminate and all references to "Company" or "Companies" in the JV Agreement (either in the body of the JV Agreement or its schedules and other attachments) shall read and be interpreted to mean VNBJ and/or ANRA except as the context otherwise requires.

c.Except as expressly set forth in this Amendment and notwithstanding anything to the contrary contained in the JV Agreement (including Section 9.3.11 (Termination and Survival)), (a) effective as of the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement shall terminate in its entirety with respect to the Veoneer Parties and (b) after the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the Veoneer Parties shall cease to be a party to the JV Agreement and shall have no further obligations with respect thereto. For clarity, upon the later of the VNBJ Closing and the VNBZ Closing, the JV Agreement will remain in full force and effect in accordance with the terms thereof solely between the Nissin Parties and Autoliv AB with respect to the liquidation proceedings of ANRA.
confidentiality_clause: Not found in this contract
liability_clause:

Article 2.D&O Indemnity

Notwithstanding Article 1.1, Article 1.2 and Article 1.3 above, the Nissin Parties agree to cause each of VNBJ and VNBZ to comply with Sections 4.3.2 and 4.3.3 of the JV Agreement with respect to the indemnification or reimbursement, as applicable, of all Directors and Officers (except those individuals resigning pursuant to Section 6.2.6 of the VNBJ SPA and Section 6.2.6 of the VNBZ SPA) with respect to any Liabilities arising prior to the VNBJ Closing or the VNBZ Closing, as applicable.

summary:
This Co-Promotion Agreement, dated September 26, 2018, establishes a collaboration between Dova Pharmaceuticals and Valeant Pharmaceuticals (Salix) to promote the product DOPTELET for the treatment of thrombocytopenia in adult patients with chronic liver disease. Under the contract, Valeant is obligated to deploy at least 100 sales representatives to perform "Detailing" activities specifically targeting gastroenterology professionals. Dova retains responsibility for product manufacturing, regulatory interactions, and final approval of all marketing materials.

Financial compensation includes a tiered promotion fee based on Net Sales and a one-time milestone payment. The agreement outlines strict compliance, non-solicitation, and non-competition requirements for both parties. Risks include potential termination for cause if performance metrics—such as minimum detail counts or sales force size—are not met. Additionally, both parties provide mutual indemnification for claims arising from negligence, misconduct, or breach of contract, while agreeing to mutual liability limitations.

contract_title: PACIRA PHARMACEUTICALS, INC. - A_R STRATEGIC LICENSING, DISTRIBUTION AND MARKETING AGREEMENT
summary:
This agreement is an Amended and Restated Strategic Licensing, Distribution and Marketing Agreement between Pacira Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (PPI) and EKR Therapeutics, Inc. (EKR) concerning the pharmaceutical product "DepoDur."

Key obligations include PPI granting EKR exclusive rights to market, distribute, and sell the product in specified territories. PPI remains responsible for manufacturing the product, while EKR assumes responsibility for maintaining regulatory authorizations and marketing. The parties established a Commercialization Committee to manage strategic decisions. Financial terms include milestone payments, royalties, and a sale-leaseback arrangement for manufacturing equipment.

Risks and liabilities are governed by indemnification clauses requiring each party to defend the other against third-party claims arising from their respective periods of operation or breaches of contract. Both parties face liability caps, excluding specific cases like gross negligence or fraud. The contract includes non-compete provisions and specific protocols for dispute resolution and termination.

summary:
This Remarketing Agreement, dated August 26, 2014, establishes the terms for Deutsche Bank Securities Inc. to remarket two tranches of MetLife, Inc. Series E Senior Component Debentures. The agreement governs the procedure for resetting interest rates and successfully selling the securities to adjust their maturity dates to 2017 and 2044, respectively.

MetLife is obligated to provide necessary disclosures and regulatory filings, while the Remarketing Agents must use commercially reasonable efforts to remarket the securities at specified prices and manage the settlement process. Both parties provide reciprocal indemnification for losses arising from material misstatements or omissions in disclosure documents. The agreement outlines strict conditions for a "Successful Remarketing," including the sale of all targeted securities by a specific deadline. If these conditions are not met, the remarketing fails, potentially impacting the issuer's obligations and the agents' duties.
termination_clause: 11. Termination of Remarketing Agreement. In addition, the obligations of the Remarketing Agents may be terminated by them by notice given to the Company prior to 12:00 noon, New York City time on the Remarketing Settlement Date if, prior to that time, any of the applicable conditions precedent to the obligations of the Remarketing Agents described in Section 5 hereof shall have failed to occur.

summary:
This Strategic Alliance Agreement between EDGE Communications Solutions, LLC and FTE Networks, Inc. establishes a subcontracting framework for telecommunications projects, including route feasibility, site acquisition, and construction services. The contract functions as an “as-ordered” agreement, where specific project requirements and compensation are defined through individual Purchase Orders.

FTE is obligated to furnish all labor, materials, and supervision necessary for assigned work, maintaining professional standards, appropriate insurance coverage, and compliance with all laws and safety regulations. FTE must also indemnify EDGE against claims or liens.

Risks include EDGE’s right to terminate the contract for cause—such as failure to perform, payment defaults to lower-tier subcontractors, or insolvency—with costs of completion deducted from unpaid balances. Furthermore, the agreement imposes strict non-solicitation clauses with liquidated damages, potential liability for defective work for up to one year, and exclusive legal venue in Collin County, Texas.

summary:
This Sponsorship Agreement, effective February 4, 2005, establishes an arrangement where Domini Social Investments LLC ("Sponsor") provides oversight, administrative, and management services to the Domini Advisor Trust. The Sponsor is responsible for maintaining organizational records, managing regulatory compliance, preparing reports, coordinating third-party contractors, and responding to shareholder inquiries. Notably, the agreement explicitly excludes investment management, asset supervision, and distribution duties from the Sponsor's scope. 

The Trust compensates the Sponsor with an annual fee of 0.50% of its average daily net assets. Regarding risks, the Sponsor’s liability is strictly limited; it is not held responsible for errors of judgment or mistakes of law unless they result from willful misfeasance, bad faith, gross negligence, or reckless disregard of duties. Either party may terminate the agreement without penalty by providing 60 days' written notice.
termination_clause: This Agreement may be terminated at any time, without the payment of any penalty, with respect to any series or the Trust, by the Board of Trustees of the Trust, or by the Sponsor, in each case on not less than 60 days' written notice to the other party.

As used in this Section 4, the term "Sponsor" shall include Domini and/or any of its affiliates and the directors, officers and employees of Domini and/or any of its affiliates.

summary:
This Non-Disclosure and Non-Competition Agreement is between a "Pizza Fusion" franchisee and its personnel. Its purpose is to protect the franchisor’s proprietary operating system and trade secrets by restricting employees from disclosing confidential information or engaging in competitive activities.

Under the agreement, the employee is prohibited from using confidential information for any purpose other than the franchised business. Furthermore, the employee must not operate, consult for, or be employed by any competing pizza business during their employment. Post-employment, these restrictions apply for two years within a three-mile radius of any active or under-construction "Pizza Fusion" location.

Breaching these covenants constitutes irreparable harm, entitling the franchisor to seek injunctive relief. The employee is liable for all associated court costs and legal fees. If any provision is deemed unenforceable, courts may reform the contract to the maximum extent legally permissible.
termination_clause: Not found in this contract

All information, knowledge and know-how relating to Franchisor, its business plans, Franchised Businesses, or the System ("Confidential Information") is deemed confidential, except for information that Member can demonstrate came to Member's attention by lawful means prior to disclosure to Member; or which, at the time of the disclosure to Member, had become a part of the public domain.
liability_clause: Member acknowledges that any failure to comply with the requirements of this Agreement will cause Franchisor irreparable injury, and Member agrees to pay all court costs and reasonable attorney's fees incurred by Franchisor in obtaining specific performance of, or an injunction against violation of, the requirements of this Agreement.

summary:
This Intellectual Property Agreement between Nuance Communications and Cerence Inc. governs the allocation, licensing, and management of intellectual property rights following the separation and spin-off of Cerence. Its primary purpose is to formalize the transfer of ownership of specified IP assets and establish mutual, perpetual, non-exclusive cross-licensing arrangements for patents, technology, and data required for each party's respective business fields.

Each party is obligated to facilitate the formal recordation of assigned IP rights and must maintain strict confidentiality regarding shared technical information. Key risks include a comprehensive disclaimer of warranties, where assets are provided on an "as is" basis, placing all operational and legal risks on the licensees. Furthermore, the agreement imposes strict limitations on liability, excluding indirect or consequential damages, and prohibits the unauthorized transfer of rights or reverse engineering of shared technology assets.

summary: This endorsement serves as a legal amendment confirming that Thrivent Financial for Lutherans has assumed all assets, liabilities, and obligations of the dissolved Thrivent Life Insurance Company. The document formally updates the contract entity, definitions, and administrative contact information, establishing the agreement as a policy with a fraternal benefit society. Regarding key obligations, the society assumes the role of the insurer, while the policyholder retains membership rights as defined by the society's bylaws. A significant risk involves the "Maintenance of Solvency" provision: if the society’s financial solvency becomes impaired, the policyholder may be mandated to pay a proportional share of the deficiency. Failure to pay within 60 days results in this amount being recorded as contract indebtedness, accruing interest at 5% annually. Additionally, while the contract is eligible for dividends, none are currently expected to be credited.

summary:
This Master Supply Agreement between Reynolds Consumer Products LLC (Seller) and Pactiv LLC (Buyer), effective November 1, 2019, establishes a framework for the sale and purchase of products. The agreement is non-exclusive; specific product types, quantities, and pricing are defined only through signed "Purchase Schedules." Both parties are obligated to maintain specific insurance coverage, comply with established operating procedures, and safeguard confidential information. 

Regarding risks and penalties, the agreement includes mutual indemnification for third-party claims arising from breaches, negligence, or intellectual property infringement. Buyer faces a 2% monthly late fee for overdue payments, and both parties waive rights to consequential or punitive damages. While the contract allows for force majeure events, it limits liability strictly to direct damages. Disputes must follow a mandatory process of good-faith negotiation, then mediation, before litigation in Illinois courts.

summary:

Integrity is obligated to solicit orders for shares, conduct promotional activities, maintain compliance with securities laws, and provide shareholder support. The Fund must provide accurate registration documentation and necessary financial information. Integrity assumes responsibility for its own distribution expenses, while the Fund compensates Integrity through sales price excesses and fees authorized under the Shareholder Services Plan.

Risks include potential liabilities arising from inaccuracies in registration statements or unauthorized marketing. The contract contains robust reciprocal indemnification clauses for such events. Either party may terminate the agreement via written notice, and liability is limited to the Fund’s assets, excluding personal liability for trustees or officers.

summary:
This Intellectual Property Agreement establishes a collaboration between Marv Enterprises, Premier Biomedical, and Technology Health, Inc. (THI) to develop and commercialize COVID-19 treatment technologies. The contract facilitates the assignment of exclusive licensing rights for specific patents and applications from Premier to THI, while Marv retains ownership of the underlying intellectual property.

THI is obligated to pay various license fees, reimburse intellectual property prosecution costs, and provide quarterly royalty payments of 5% on sales and services derived from the licensed products. Additionally, THI must fund specific development milestones for the joint venture. Failure to meet these financial obligations triggers a penalty: the reversion of exclusive license rights back to Premier. The agreement also enforces strict confidentiality requirements regarding proprietary information and stipulates that any overdue payments incur interest charges based on the prime rate. All legal disputes are subject to Pennsylvania law.

summary: This Strategic Alliance Agreement establishes a partnership between Bravatek Solutions, Inc. and Sibannac, Inc. to promote Sibannac’s patented Air Cylinder Wheel technology to government and non-government entities. Bravatek acts as a non-exclusive project sales lead finder, responsible for marketing, pipeline reporting, and client engagement. The agreement spans an initial twelve-month term, with automatic renewal unless terminated with ninety days' written notice, and is governed by the laws of Texas.

summary:
This Strategic Alliance Agreement between IntriCon Corporation and Dynamic Hearing Pty Ltd establishes a framework for collaborating on digital signal processing (DSP) technology within the hearing health, assisted listening, and professional communication markets. 

The agreement grants IntriCon a license to use Dynamic Hearing’s proprietary technology and software for manufacturing and selling products. IntriCon is obligated to pay annual technology access fees and escalating minimum payments to maintain exclusivity for hearing aids, alongside per-unit royalties based on sales volume. 

The contract contains significant financial risks, including strict payment deadlines and auditing requirements. Both parties are subject to confidentiality obligations and broad indemnification clauses covering third-party infringements or breaches. Dynamic Hearing limits its aggregate liability to $5 million, and both parties exclude consequential damages. The agreement is governed by the laws of Victoria, Australia, and remains in effect for five years.

summary:
This Agency Agreement, effective April 18, 2018, formalizes the liquidation of assets for The Bon-Ton Stores, Inc. and its affiliated debtors ("Merchant"). The Agent (GA Retail, Inc. and Tiger Capital Group, LLC) and Notes Trustee are appointed to conduct "going out of business" sales and dispose of inventory, fixtures, intellectual property, and real estate leases.

Merchant’s obligations include providing access to facilities, managing payroll, and maintaining assets. The Agent is responsible for conducting sales, funding specific wind-down expenses, and paying a cash purchase price. 

The agreement limits the Merchant’s liabilities, while the Agent assumes specific costs under a "Wind-Down Budget" and maintains superpriority administrative claims. Notable risks include potential conflicts over lease assignments, limited liability protections for the Agent regarding employee claims, and the forfeiture of the $32.7 million deposit if the deal fails due to the Agent's material, non-excusable breach.
termination_clause: Agent may, in its discretion, earlier terminate the GOB Sale on a Store-by- Store basis upon not less than seven (7) days' prior written notice (a "Vacate Notice") to Merchant (the "Vacate Date"), provided, that it being understood that Agent's obligations to pay all Expenses, including Occupancy Expenses, for each Store subject to a Vacate Notice shall continue until the applicable Vacate Date, provided, however, that, with respect to Occupancy Expenses, Agent's obligations to pay all Occupancy Expenses for each Store shall continue until the last day of the calendar month in which the Vacate Date occurs for such Store.

summary:

Wildform is obligated to provide transition services, including software development (customization and integration of Flix Software), technical support, and marketing assistance over an eighteen-month term. On2 must provide necessary technology and compensate Wildform through monthly cash payments totaling $160,000, plus 280,000 shares of On2 common stock.

Key risks include strict confidentiality requirements, with potential for injunctive relief for breaches. Deliverables are provided "as-is," limiting warranties. If Wildform fails to meet specific delivery deadlines or cure material breaches within thirty days, it faces contractual penalties. Both parties are subject to indemnification obligations for breaches of confidentiality, warranties, or intellectual property rights. The agreement is governed by New York law, requiring mediation before pursuing formal court action for most disputes.

summary: This Website Design, Development, and Hosting Agreement outlines a professional service contract between Natalija Tunevic (Client) and Mitchell Vitalis (Company). The purpose is to design, develop, and host a website for the brand FreeCook for a total contract price of $5,000. Key obligations require the Client to provide proofread content in specific electronic formats and pay in two installments. The Company is responsible for delivering the project within a twelve-week timeframe, subject to defined scope requirements. Risks and penalties include additional charges for "Change Orders" involving revisions beyond two rounds, and surcharges for rush work or off-hours service requested due to Client delays. Furthermore, the Company retains the right to modify or cease customer service at its discretion without liability, and the Client grants the Company a limited license to utilize the Client’s branding for marketing and promotional purposes.

summary:

(a Chinese corporation). The contract governs the provision of system platform maintenance and data processing services to support PC Financial’s small loan operations in India.

Mobimagic is obligated to provide professional services and maintain data integrity, while PC Financial must pay a monthly fee of $970,000. Both parties are bound by strict confidentiality obligations, valid for five years post-termination.

Regarding liabilities, both parties must indemnify each other against losses resulting from breaches of the agreement or applicable laws. Total cumulative liability is capped at the aggregate fees paid for services. Either party may terminate the agreement for uncured material breaches or insolvency. Disputes are subject to mandatory arbitration in New Delhi under Indian law.

summary:
This Joint Venture Agreement establishes "BM&V2GO," a venture between BorrowMoney.com, Inc. and JVLS, LLC (Vaccines 2Go), formed under Florida law to provide IT development, back-office maintenance, and medical service deployment.

Both parties contributed property or capital valued at $3,500,000.

Risks include potential involuntary withdrawal for uncured breaches or undisclosed conflicts of interest. Members are liable for harm resulting from gross negligence or willful misconduct. The agreement mandates that disputes be resolved through mediation or binding arbitration in Florida. Failure to perform or unauthorized withdrawal may trigger indemnification obligations or the dissolution and liquidation of the venture's assets.

summary:
This Intellectual Property Agreement, dated April 14, 2014, formalizes the transfer of specific intellectual property assets from Motorola Solutions, Inc. (Seller) to Zebra Technologies Corporation (Purchaser) as part of a broader business acquisition.

The agreement mandates the transfer of "Assigned IP" to the Purchaser, while establishing cross-licensing arrangements. The Purchaser receives broad rights to use, exploit, and commercialize the assigned assets. Conversely, the Seller retains limited, non-exclusive, and perpetual licenses to utilize certain transferred technologies and trademarks for its remaining business operations.

Key risks include potential disputes over patent ownership, which are subject to a specified review process and mandatory arbitration in Chicago. Additionally, the agreement imposes strict confidentiality requirements regarding trade secrets and Source Code. Termination is restricted, though specific trademark licenses may be revoked if the Purchaser commits a material breach that remains uncured after 120 days.

summary:

(Licensor) and Beijing Yisheng Leju Information Services Co., Ltd. (Licensee). The agreement authorizes the Licensee to use specific domain names and proprietary content to operate an online real estate media platform in the PRC.

Key obligations require the Licensee to manage the websites, monitor content for illegal or politically sensitive material, and adhere to strict usage restrictions to protect the Licensor’s reputation. The Licensor remains responsible for domain registration maintenance. Risks include potential contract termination triggered by a "Change of Control," breach of compliance, or the termination of a related advertising agency agreement. Both parties are subject to mutual indemnification obligations for third-party claims arising from breaches, and unresolved disputes are subject to binding arbitration under the China International Economic and Trade Arbitration Commission.

(HERC) following their corporate split. The agreement details the ownership, licensing, and assignment of HERTZ-formative and HERC-specific intellectual property used in the Equipment Rental Business.

Key obligations include HSI granting HERC temporary, royalty-free, non-exclusive licenses for various HERTZ-related trademarks and domains during a four-year "Interim Period." HERC must eventually discontinue these uses and rename its business to remove the HERTZ mark. HERC is responsible for maintenance costs and must maintain specific liability insurance.

Risks include strict non-compete requirements and HERC’s obligation to indemnify THC and HSI against third-party claims. Failure to cure a material breach or insolvency triggers automatic termination of the agreement and immediate cessation of all licensed property usage.

summary:
This Consulting Agreement, dated January 2, 2020, engages Timothy Cabrera to provide transactional business services to Global Technologies, Ltd. The scope includes business plan development, identifying acquisition targets, negotiating mergers, managing subsidiaries, and assisting with asset sales.

The Consultant must exert reasonable efforts to perform these tasks, while the Company is obligated to provide accurate information and cooperate. Compensation is set at $250,000, structured as a reduction of a specific Convertible Note, with additional fees negotiable for future revenue.

Significant risks include the Consultant’s total forfeiture of consideration if the Company provides untruthful information. Both parties agree to mutual indemnification for negligent acts or contract breaches. The Consultant is an independent contractor responsible for all personal tax liabilities, while both parties are bound by strict non-circumvention, confidentiality, and mandatory mediation followed by binding arbitration for dispute resolution in Pinellas County, Florida.

Either Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement with notice, and the effective date of termination shall be the date such notice is received (by mail, overnight delivery, or fax) by the terminated Party.

summary:
This franchise agreement establishes a contract between Pizza Fusion Holdings, Inc. and a franchisee to operate a "Pizza Fusion" restaurant utilizing the company's proprietary "green" system and marks.

The franchisee is obligated to pay an initial $30,000 fee, a 6% weekly royalty on gross revenues, and mandatory marketing contributions of up to 5%. The franchisee must adhere to strict operational standards, maintain required insurance, secure an approved location, and ensure the "Operating Principal" completes mandatory training.

Risks include the franchisor’s reserved right to operate or license competing locations nearby, including at "institutional facilities," without offering territorial exclusivity. The contract mandates strict non-competition covenants during and for two years after the term. Penalties for default or early termination include substantial liquidated damages, loss of business assets, and mandatory reimbursement of the franchisor’s legal and administrative enforcement costs.

summary:
This License and Hosting Agreement, entered into on October 29, 1999, establishes a partnership between Corio Inc. and Commerce One, Inc. The purpose of this contract is to grant Corio a license to host and provide Commerce One’s proprietary software and "MarketSite" services to its customers as an Application Service Provider.

Key obligations include Commerce One providing the software, ongoing support, and updates, while Corio manages hosting, customer support, and sales within an agreed-upon market segment. The parties also agree to share certain revenues and cooperate on marketing efforts.

Risks and penalties involve strict intellectual property ownership by Commerce One and limitations on liability for both parties, generally capped at amounts paid in the preceding twelve months. Further, the agreement outlines specific termination conditions, audit rights for verifying revenue, and mandatory arbitration for dispute resolution in California.

This Agreement shall terminate immediately upon notice if either party becomes insolvent (i.e., becomes unable to pay its debts in the ordinary course of business as they come due) or makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors. Compliance by the Software with the Software's specifications after expiration of the Warranty Period shall be deemed a material condition of this Agreement.

THE PARTIES AGREE THAT THIS SECTION 9 REPRESENTS A REASONABLE ALLOCATION OF RISK.

summary:
This Cooperation Agreement (2014 Amendment) establishes a long-term service relationship between Nanjing Tuniu Technology Co., Ltd. (Party A) and Beijing Tuniu Technology Co., Ltd. (Party B). Its purpose is to authorize Party B to provide exclusive technical, business consultancy, and internet platform services to Party A and its subsidiaries.

Key obligations require Party A to utilize Party B as its sole, exclusive service provider, refraining from competitive arrangements with third parties. Party A must pay a quarterly service fee equal to its generated profits, which Party B may adjust at its discretion. Party B holds ownership of all intellectual property developed under the agreement.

Risks include significant financial liability, as late payments trigger liquidated damages at a daily rate of 0.03%. Party A’s termination rights are severely restricted, while Party B retains unilateral authority to terminate. Both parties are bound by strict confidentiality and non-compete clauses.
termination_clause: 6.1 Prior to the expiration of the Term of Cooperation , this Agreement shall only be terminated upon occurrence of the following circumstances: 6.1.1 Party B shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in advance without the prior written consent from Party A, by sending a written notice to Party A but Party A may not terminate or rescind this Agreement; 6.1.2 One Party requests to terminate this Agreement when the other Party is declared bankrupt in accordance with the laws; 6.1.3 Party B fails to provide Party B's Services to Party A for more than three consecutive years due to the force majeure event.
confidentiality_clause: 12.8.1 Neither Party shall disclose the financial and technical information obtained in the course of conclusion of this Agreement to any third party nor use such information for matters irrelevant to this Agreement, regardless of written or oral information, unless the other Party gives a prior written consent thereto. 12.8.2 The Parties shall be obligated to take measures (including without limitation preparing the confidentiality rules, entering into the confidentiality agreement, establishing the archive management system and etc.) to ensure their respective employees will observe the confidentiality obligations specified hereunder.

If both Parties breach this Agreement, they shall each assume their own liabilities for breach respectively. Notwithstanding the foregoing provisions, neither Party shall be responsible to the other Party in respect of any indirect loss or damage caused hereunder.

summary:
This Promotion Agreement establishes a marketing partnership between CNET, Inc. and Cyberian Outpost, Inc. The purpose is to facilitate product sales through CNET’s provision of television and online promotional advertising, including banners and retail impressions, across its network of sites.

Key obligations require CNET to deliver specific promotional impressions and report performance metrics. Cyberian Outpost must maintain its retail site to defined performance standards, provide monthly sales reports, pay a recurring cash fee plus a percentage of generated sales, and implement reciprocal marketing links.

Significant risks include material breach provisions regarding site performance and reporting accuracy. Both parties provide mutual indemnification for third-party intellectual property or operational claims. Liability is limited by excluding consequential damages. The agreement allows for audits, and CNET is mandated to fulfill any shortfall in promised impressions even if the contract term expires.
termination_clause: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") will begin on February 1, 1998 and end on the first anniversary of the date of this Agreement; provided that (a) either party may terminate this Agreement, effective at any time after the first three months of the Term, by giving 30 days' written notice of termination to the other party, and (b) either party may terminate this Agreement at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party, if the other party commits a material breach of its obligations hereunder that is not cured within 30 days after notice thereof from the non-breaching party. If this Agreement is terminated during any of the three month periods referenced in Section 2.3.2, then the required number of Retail Impressions applicable thereunder to such three month period will be pro rated accordingly.
confidentiality_clause: The material terms of this agreement and any information exchanged in connection herewith shall be covered by the Non-Disclosure Agreement between CNET and the Company dated December 5, 1997 (the "NDA"). Notwithstanding the foregoing the following information will not be considered "Confidential Information" for purposes of the NDA provided that such information is not publicly identified as belonging to or coming from the Company: (a) information contained in the reports described in Section 3.2, (b) the names and e-mail addresses referenced in Section 3.4 and (c) any information obtained by CNET from Users who affirmatively request to be added to an e-mail newsletter pursuant to Section 3.5.
liability_clause: NEITHER PARTY WILL BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, CONSEQUENTIAL OR INCIDENTAL DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, HOWEVER CAUSED AND ON ANY THEORY OF LIABILITY (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), AND EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

summary:
This Collaboration Agreement between Didi Chuxing Technology Co., Ltd. (Party A) and Hunan Ruixi Financial Leasing Co., Ltd. (Party B) establishes a framework for Party B to provide vehicle financial leasing services to drivers on Didi’s ride-hailing platform.

Party B is obligated to supply vehicles that meet safety and regulatory standards, maintain high-quality leasing schemes, and adhere to strict platform rules, including the use of specific financial leasing templates. Party A provides platform access, maintains the system, and reserves the right to audit Party B’s materials and operations.

Risks and penalties are significant; Party A may impose liquidated damages ranging from RMB 1,000 to RMB 50,000 or more, depending on the severity of the breach. Party B must maintain a performance deposit, which Party A may deduct for violations. The agreement also contains strict anti-bribery and confidentiality clauses, with material breaches potentially leading to contract termination.

summary:
This non-exclusive Reseller Agreement appoints Inde Living Holdings, Inc. to market and sell Vayyar Imaging Ltd.’s "Walabot-HOME" products within the United States. The contract defines the terms for product distribution, including branding requirements, mandatory training, and the obligation to ensure end users sign license agreements.

Key obligations include the Reseller meeting specified minimum order quantities (MOQ). Failure to meet the MOQ results in a penalty payment equal to the shortfall. The Reseller bears full liability for end-user non-compliance with license terms and must indemnify the Supplier against third-party claims arising from the Reseller’s conduct or breaches. The Products are sold "as is," and the Supplier explicitly disclaims liability for indirect or consequential damages. The agreement lasts one year with automatic annual renewals, and includes provisions for termination due to material breach or insolvency.
termination_clause: Following the Initial Term, either Party may terminate this Agreement without cause upon written notice to the other Party of at least 3 months. Without derogating from any other remedies that any Party may have under the terms of this Agreement or at law, each Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement forthwith upon the occurrence of any of the following: (i) the other Party is in material breach of its obligations hereunder and fails to remedy such breach within 30 days after being requested in writing to do so; or (ii) the other Party's bankruptcy, insolvency, liquidation, whether voluntarily or otherwise, or if it makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors.

Each Party shall: (i) safeguard and keep secret all Confidential Information, and will not directly or indirectly disclose to any third party the Confidential Information without written permission of the other; and (ii) in performing its obligations hereunder, use at least the same degree of care as it does with respect to its own confidential information of like importance but, in any event, at least reasonable care. Any information related to the Products shall be considered the Supplier's Confidential Information. If either Party becomes legally obliged to make disclosure of the other Party's Confidential Information, that Party shall immediately notify the first Party and reasonably cooperate with the first Party in any efforts to limit or object to the disclosure.

summary:
This Stored Value Products Reseller Agreement, effective August 1, 2004, appoints Schoolpop, Inc. as an exclusive reseller of American Express Incentive Services (AEIS) stored value cards within the nonprofit organization (NPO) marketplace. 

Schoolpop is obligated to meet an annual $52,000,000 funding minimum to maintain exclusivity, pay marketing and product fees, and manage inventory under strict security protocols. Schoolpop must provide detailed sales forecasts, maintain specific Card "life" periods, and comply with all anti-money laundering and privacy laws. 

Risks and penalties include substantial financial liabilities for lost or misused cards, inventory penalties for forecast inaccuracies, and customer service penalties based on call volumes. AEIS may revoke exclusivity or terminate the agreement if Schoolpop fails to meet funding requirements, defaults on security audits, or distributes unapproved promotional materials. Both parties are subject to indemnification requirements and strict confidentiality regarding proprietary data.

Upon the occurrence of a breach by the other party, which breach has not been cured within (30) days after the date of written notice to the breaching party by the non-breaching party; or iii. For non-payment of any amounts due hereunder.

Accordingly, both parties agree to receive and hold in confidence all Confidential Information considered proprietary or confidential by the other party, and agree not to disclose or use such information in any manner except for uses contemplated by this Agreement unless permitted in writing in advance by the other party (except for information disclosed to Cardholders in accordance with the Cardholder Agreement). Both parties also agree to bind their employees, and subcontractors to compliance with this paragraph and to take all other reasonable action to ensure protection of the Confidential Information.

In no event shall either party be liable to the other, under any theory, for lost profits, exemplary, punitive, special incidental, indirect, or consequential damages.

summary:
This Licensing and Web Site Hosting Agreement, effective February 26, 1999, establishes a business relationship between Mortgage Logic.com, Inc. ("Client") and TrueLink, Inc. ("TrueLink"). The agreement governs TrueLink’s provision of interface software, hosting services, and technical support, allowing the Client to access automated underwriting systems and credit bureau data.

Key obligations include TrueLink providing hosting, server maintenance, data backups, and technical programming support. In exchange, the Client must pay monthly service fees and adhere to usage restrictions, including a prohibition on "spamming." Both parties are bound by strict confidentiality requirements regarding proprietary information.

Significant risks involve mutual indemnification clauses and liability limitations for TrueLink, which cap damages at the previous year's compensation, except in cases of gross negligence. The agreement is subject to California law, with potential termination for contractual breaches or failure to maintain compliance with third-party system providers.
termination_clause: This Agreement will take effect on the Effective Date and remain in effect for a period of 1 year; provided, that Sections 2 and 3 level shall terminate promptly upon (a) any action or omission by Client or any of its customers which constitutes a breach of or default by TrueLink under any System Agreement or Credit Repository Agreement, which breach or default has either not been cured or cannot be cured within the applicable cure period and the consequences of which is that TrueLink will lose material rights it had pursuant to said Agreements or (b) any violations or breach by Client of Sections 8a - 8c. This Agreement shall renew automatically thereafter for successive one year periods until terminated pursuant to Section 12 herein or unless either Client or TrueLink deliver to the other written notice of intent not to renew no later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of said year. Subject to Section 4 hereof, TrueLink will continue to provide the requested Hosting Services and Support Services until the last day of the month following the month in which Client provides TrueLink with a written notice of its election to terminate this Agreement.
confidentiality_clause: Each agrees to treat all of the other Proprietary Information and all materials as strictly confidential, except to the extent otherwise agreed by the other in writing. Except to the extent otherwise agreed by both parties in writing, party further agrees to treat all of the other Proprietary Information and all materials which it prepares using or based on the other's Proprietary information or any portion thereof (the "Derivative Documentation") as strictly confidential, including, without limitation, any notes made and all reports prepared in connection with this Agreement.

summary:
This Strategic Alliance Agreement between Dialog Semiconductor (UK) Ltd and Energous Corporation establishes a partnership to distribute and commercialize Energous’s wire-free charging technology. Dialog receives a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-bearing license to manufacture, market, and sell Licensed Products.

Key obligations include Energous providing technical support, documentation, and manufacturing authorizations, while Dialog manages sales operations and product forecasting. The parties must collaborate on a commercialization plan and maintain defined service levels. Both parties commit to non-solicitation of employees and specific branding requirements.

Significant risks include defined liabilities for "Epidemic Defects," which are capped at a specific monetary limit per occurrence. Additionally, the contract outlines strict indemnification obligations for third-party intellectual property infringement and product liability. Termination rights are triggered by material breaches, insolvency, or defined changes of control, with a transitionary "Wind Down Period" for ongoing customer obligations.
termination_clause: 15.1 Term. This Agreement is effective on the Effective Date. Unless earlier terminated as provided herein, this Agreement continues in effect for an initial term of seven (7) years ("Initial Term") and will automatically renew for one or more annual periods after the Initial Term (each a "Renewal Term") unless either party gives notice of non-renewal at least one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the beginning of any Renewal Term. 15.2 Termination. (a) Mutual Termination Rights. Either party may, in addition to any other remedies available to it under this Agreement or at law or in equity, terminate this Agreement (or, in the event this Agreement has been previously terminated, the Wind Down Period, if any) immediately upon the issuance of written notice to the other party in the event that (i) the other party materially breaches a material provision of this Agreement, and fails to cure such breach within thirty (30) days, or (ii) the other party undergoes an Insolvency Event. (b) Termination By ENERGOUS... (c) Termination by DIALOG...

summary:
This Commercialization and License Agreement, effective December 17, 2019, establishes an exclusive partnership between Vyera Pharmaceuticals, LLC and CytoDyn Inc. regarding the commercialization of leronlimab (PRO 140) for HIV treatment in the United States.

CytoDyn retains responsibility for drug development, manufacturing, and regulatory filings, while Vyera is exclusively responsible for commercialization activities, including marketing and distribution, within the defined field and territory. Vyera must adhere to agreed-upon "Minimum Requirements" and commercialization plans.

Key risks include stringent termination clauses: CytoDyn may terminate the agreement for material breaches, failure to meet minimum investment requirements, or if Vyera challenges CytoDyn’s patents. Vyera may terminate for commercial failure or specific regulatory delays. Both parties are bound by strict confidentiality obligations, and the contract specifies limitations on liability and mandatory procedures for dispute resolution, including potential arbitration for non-reserved disputes.

summary:
This Intellectual Property Agreement, effective November 20, 2007, governs the cross-licensing of specific generic technology assets between Morgan Stanley (MS) and MSCI Inc. following their separation. The purpose is to facilitate the continued use of shared hardware configurations, generic software libraries, and document templates utilized prior to the "Trigger Date."

Both parties grant each other a non-exclusive, perpetual, irrevocable, and royalty-free license to use, modify, and sublicense these assets for any business purpose. Critically, there is no obligation for either party to provide maintenance, support, or delivery of the materials. The materials are provided on an "as-is" basis, with all warranties, including merchantability and non-infringement, expressly disclaimed. The agreement excludes broad liability for both parties, specifically prohibiting claims for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages, and stipulates that New York law governs all disputes, which are subject to mandatory jury trial waivers.

summary:
This Manufacturing Outsourcing Agreement, effective January 21, 2002, establishes a turnkey manufacturing arrangement between Nice Systems Ltd. and Flextronics Israel Ltd. The Contractor is obligated to purchase components, assemble, test, and deliver digital recording products per Nice’s forecasts and quality specifications. Conversely, Nice must issue purchase orders, manage software licensing, and provide necessary infrastructure and inventory support.

Key risks include potential supply chain disruptions and product quality failures, for which the Contractor bears primary responsibility. The contract mandates strict delivery timelines; failure to meet these deadlines triggers liquidated damages capped at 5% of the product price. Both parties are subject to liability limits of $5,000,000 for indirect or consequential damages. Additional risks involve potential intellectual property infringement claims and termination penalties, with Nice retaining the right to terminate for convenience upon 45 days' written notice.

summary:
This Co-Branding Agreement, effective March 3, 2000, establishes a partnership between VerticalNet, Inc. and Impresse Corporation to create a co-branded website integrating Impresse’s commercial printing services with VerticalNet’s online industry communities.

Key obligations include joint design, hosting, and maintenance of the co-branded site, with Impresse providing printing services and VerticalNet providing advertising exposure through banners, newsletters, and specialized links. Impresse is required to pay development, slotting, and hosting fees, alongside a revenue-sharing model based on registered users.

Risks and liabilities are strictly defined; both parties provide limited warranties, disclaim implied guarantees, and cap liability for damages, excluding specific confidentiality breaches or intellectual property infringement. The contract includes mandatory arbitration for disputes, strict confidentiality requirements regarding proprietary business data and user information, and mutual indemnification obligations for third-party claims arising from breaches, negligence, or intellectual property violations.

summary:
This Joint Content License Agreement, dated February 1, 2018, establishes a cooperative marketing and branding partnership between WPT Enterprises, Inc. and Zynga. The contract governs a worldwide licensing arrangement—excluding specific Asian territories—whereby both parties agree to promote each other’s poker-related products and services.

Key obligations require Zynga to integrate a WPT-branded tournament mode into its platforms and pay a $3,000,000 annual minimum guarantee, recoupable against a 10% royalty on net revenues derived from the WPT-branded content. Conversely, WPT must promote Zynga within its televised poker tournaments. 

Regarding risks, the agreement includes mutual indemnification for third-party claims arising from breaches or legal non-compliance. Both parties explicitly waive rights to equitable or injunctive relief and limit liability for consequential damages, except in cases of gross negligence, fraud, or specific confidentiality and intellectual property breaches. The contract remains subject to audit rights and defined termination clauses.

summary:
This Strategic Alliance Agreement establishes a formal partnership between Freedom Mortgage Corporation and its affiliate, Cherry Hill Mortgage Investment Corp. The contract serves to leverage Freedom Mortgage’s origination and servicing capabilities alongside Cherry Hill’s access to capital, creating a consistent pipeline of real estate assets.

Key obligations include executing an Acknowledgement Agreement with Ginnie Mae and entering into ancillary purchase and flow agreements. Freedom Mortgage must also designate a standby issuer if specific financial or operational "Standby Trigger Events" occur.

Regarding risks, the agreement mandates strict financial performance benchmarks for Freedom Mortgage. Failure to meet these, or a material breach of contract, allows for termination after a 30-day cure period. Freedom Mortgage is specifically required to indemnify Cherry Hill against losses resulting from Ginnie Mae’s termination of Freedom Mortgage’s status as an issuer. Disputes are subject to specific New York jurisdiction and jury trial waivers.
termination_clause:

(b) In the event that a party materially breaches any representation or covenant herein, the other party may give written notice of the breach requiring the same to be remedied within 30 days of receipt of such notice. If the breaching party fails to remedy the material breach in such time period, the non-breaching party may terminate this Agreement by delivery of a written termination notice to the breaching party. Any such termination shall not relieve the breaching party from any obligation or liability arising prior to such termination.

summary:
This Chase Affiliate Agreement governs the contractual relationship between Chase Bank and third-party Affiliates who market Chase credit cards via website links. Affiliates must register and receive approval to participate.

The primary obligation of the Affiliate is to promote Chase products ethically using only approved content, links, and trademarks while avoiding restricted behaviors like spamming, domain squatting, or unauthorized telemarketing. Chase agrees to pay commissions for each "Approved Account" resulting from these links.

Risks and penalties are significant; Chase maintains sole discretion to reject or terminate the agreement for any "Restricted Content" or violations of program policies. Affiliates are prohibited from using specific restricted keywords and must indemnify Chase against losses arising from site content or contractual breaches. Furthermore, Chase may modify agreement terms at any time via website posting, with continued participation constituting binding acceptance of those changes.
termination_clause: The term of this Agreement will commence on the date that the Affiliate Registration Form is approved by Chase and will end when terminated by either party. Either Affiliate or Chase may terminate this Agreement at any time, with or without cause, by giving the other party written or e-mail notice of termination. At the time of termination, any links to Chase's Website must be removed immediately. Affiliate will continue to receive Commission payments for all Approved Accounts placed during the term of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Chase may terminate this Agreement if Affiliate does not comply with the terms and conditions herein.
confidentiality_clause: Except as otherwise provided in this Agreement or with the consent of the other party hereto, each of the parties hereto agrees that all information including, without limitation, the terms of this Agreement, business and financial information, customer and vendor lists, and pricing and sales information, concerning Chase, Customer or Affiliate shall remain strictly confidential and secret and shall not be utilized, directly or indirectly, by such party for its own business purposes or for any other purpose except and solely to the extent necessary to exercise rights and perform obligations under this Agreement. The foregoing restrictions will not apply to information to the extent it (i) was known to the receiving party at the time of disclosure; (ii) has become publicly known through no wrongful act of the receiving party; (iii) has been rightfully received from a third party under no obligation to the disclosing party; (iv) has been disclosed by court order or as otherwise required by law if the receiving party has given the disclosing party a reasonable opportunity to contest or limit the scope of such required disclosure.
liability_clause: Chase shall have no liability for any indirect, incidental, special or consequential damages or any loss of revenue or profits arising under or with respect to this Agreement or the Affiliate Program, regardless of whether Chase has been advised of the possibility of such damages. Further, Chase's aggregate liability arising under or with respect to this Agreement or the Affiliate Program shall in no event exceed the total Commissions paid or payable by Chase to Affiliate under this Agreement.

summary:
This Web Hosting Agreement establishes a service partnership between Galacticomm and Horst Entertainment Inc., superseding all prior arrangements. Regarding obligations, Horst Entertainment must pay Galacticomm for web access on a tiered fee structure: $0.01 per access up to 400,000, and $0.005 per access thereafter, payable weekly. Both parties are mutually obligated to maintain 24/7 technical support accessibility via telephone or beeper to address server or broadcast site downtime immediately. 

Concerning risks and liabilities, the agreement lacks specific penalty clauses for service failures but dictates a clear contingency for contract termination. Should Galacticomm elect to terminate the agreement, Horst Entertainment retains the right to acquire a licensed copy of the software for $15,000. Finally, the contract mandates that all legal disputes arising from these terms must be litigated exclusively within the court system of Florida.
termination_clause: In the event Galacticomm, Inc. chooses to terminate this agreement, Horst Entertainment Inc. will have the right to purchase a license copy of the software in the amount of $15,000.00.
confidentiality_clause: Not found in this contract
liability_clause: Not found in this contract

contract_title: Zounds Hearing, Inc. - MANUFACTURING DESIGN MARKETING AGREEMENT
summary:
This Manufacturing, Design, and Marketing Agreement establishes a ten-year partnership between Zounds Hearing, Inc. (Subcontractor) and InnerScope Hearing Technologies, Inc. (Manufacturer). The agreement governs the provision of design, manufacturing, and supply chain services for hearing aids, the lease of an FDA-registered facility, and joint marketing efforts.

The Manufacturer must pay a $1,000,000 technology access fee, recurring product costs, and royalties per unit. The Subcontractor is responsible for manufacturing products to specifications and maintaining regulatory compliance. 

Significant risks include non-cancellable purchase orders and strict limitations on liability, as neither party is liable for consequential or punitive damages. The Manufacturer assumes all product warranty and third-party royalty risks. Furthermore, termination triggers specific financial penalties, including potential cancellation charges. The agreement also includes stringent non-solicitation clauses with heavy liquidated damages and mandates a formal dispute resolution process through negotiation and mediation before litigation.

All orders placed under this Agreement must be placed prior to the expiration or termination of this Agreement.

summary:
This Servicing Agreement, dated April 8, 2020, establishes a contract between CURO Receivables Finance II, LLC (Owner) and CURO Management, LLC (Servicer). The agreement appoints the Servicer as an independent contractor to manage, administer, and collect on specified assets, including receivables and participation interests, in accordance with the "Servicing Standard" of reasonable care and applicable law.

Key obligations include the Servicer’s duty to maintain accurate records, perform administrative tasks, generate required reports, and process collections. The Owner is required to pay a specified Servicing Fee and provide necessary documentation. 

Risks and liabilities are addressed through reciprocal indemnification: the Owner indemnifies the Servicer, and the Servicer indemnifies the Owner, excluding cases of gross negligence, bad faith, or willful misconduct. The agreement outlines specific termination rights for the Owner and Agent, particularly in the event of default, bankruptcy, or failure to maintain necessary licenses.
termination_clause:
Section 7. Term of Agreement; Resignation and Removal of Servicer.

(a) This Agreement shall continue in force until the earlier to occur of (i) the Owner no longer owns any Receivables or Participation Interests, and (ii) subject to Section 7(d), the delivery of written notice of termination by the Owner to the Servicer pursuant to Section 7(c), in each case upon which event this Agreement shall automatically terminate unless otherwise agreed in writing between the Servicer and the Owner.

(b) Subject to Section 7(d), the Servicer may resign its duties hereunder by providing the Owner with at least 60 days' prior written notice.

(c) Subject to Section 7(d), and subject to the prior written consent of the Agent so long as the Loan Agreement remains outstanding, the Owner may remove the Servicer immediately upon written notice of termination from the Owner to the Servicer if any of the following events shall occur (each, a "Servicer Termination Right - Owner"):

(i) the Servicer shall default in the performance of any of its duties under this Agreement and, after notice of such default, shall not cure such default within 30 days (or, if such default cannot be cured in such time, shall not give within 30 days such assurance of cure as shall be reasonably satisfactory to the Owner); or

(ii) the Servicer files or consents to the filing of any petition, either voluntary or involuntary, to take advantage of any applicable insolvency, bankruptcy, liquidation or reorganization statute, or makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors; or

(iii) the Servicer fails to maintain, in any material respect, all necessary licenses and approvals in each jurisdiction in which it is performing the primary servicing function for any of the Serviced Assets under this Agreement.

(e) Agent may terminate this Agreement upon the occurrence and continuance of an Event of Default under the Loan Agreement by delivery of written notice of termination from Agent to Owner and Servicer (a "Servicer Termination Right - Agent" and, together with the Servicer Termination Right - Owner, each a "Servicer Termination Right")
confidentiality_clause: In no case may Servicer use any identifiable information, including consumers' nonpublic personal information and related account performance and status information, for any purpose other than as provided in this Agreement; provided, however, that Servicer is permitted to use nonidentifiable, aggregated consumer information obtained in connection with its activities undertaken pursuant to this Agreement.
liability_clause:
Section 13. Limitation of Liability; Indemnification.

(a) Except as provided in Section 13(b), neither the Servicer nor any of the directors, officers, partners, members, managers, employees, or agents of the Servicer in its capacity as Servicer shall be under any liability to the Owner or any other Person for any action taken or for refraining from the taking of any action in good faith in its capacity as Servicer in accordance with this Agreement; provided, however, that this provision shall not protect the Servicer or any such Person against contractual liability under this Agreement for any breach of warranties or representations made herein, or any failure to perform any express contractual duties set forth herein, or any liability which would otherwise be imposed by reason of willful misfeasance, bad faith or negligence in the performance of its duties hereunder.

(b) Subject to Section 13(a), the Servicer shall indemnify and hold harmless the Owner, the Agent, each Lender and their respective directors, officers, employees, partners, members or managers and agents (each, an "Indemnified Person") from and against any and all loss, liability, claim, action, suit, cost, expense, damage or injury, of any kind and nature whatsoever, including any judgment, award, settlement, fines, reasonable attorneys' fees and other costs or expenses incurred in connection with the defense of any action, Proceeding, investigation or claim (any of the foregoing, "Losses") suffered or sustained by any of them by reason of any acts or omissions of the Servicer which are in breach of this Agreement or which arise by reason of willful misfeasance, bad faith or negligence in the Servicer's performance of its duties hereunder; provided that the Servicer shall not be obligated to indemnify any such Indemnified Person for any Losses that arise from the negligence or willful misconduct of such Indemnified Person or its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, partners, members, managers or agents.

summary:
This agreement outlines the general terms for an affiliate marketing program managed by element 5. It facilitates partnerships between software publishers and web-based affiliates to promote and sell software products.

Affiliates are responsible for integrating tracking links on their websites and providing accurate product descriptions; they receive commission based on generated sales. Software publishers control affiliate activation and set commission rates, while element 5 manages the technical sales infrastructure, payment processing, and administrative services for a 2% service fee.

Risks include the potential forfeiture of commissions for non-functional links, cancelled orders, or chargebacks. Affiliates bear significant legal risk through comprehensive indemnification clauses, holding them liable for any content breaches or third-party infringement on their sites. Liability for errors or service interruptions is largely excluded. Either party may terminate the agreement at any time, requiring the immediate removal of all promotional material.

This Agreement is entered into for an unlimited period of time. Either party may cancel this Agreement at any time, with or without supplying a reason, through written notification or by making suitable settings in the respective Control Panel.

This includes in particular the liability exclusion for lost profit, the loss of data or interruption to or errors in the operation of the Web site of the Affiliate. The above liability exclusion also applies to the personal liability of employees, representatives and persons employed to perform the obligations of the parties to the agreement. The limitation of liability does not apply to claims arising through initial incapacity or in case of justifiable impossibility. (2) Any possible product liability claims are unaffected by the above limitations. Insofar as element 5 or the Software Publisher violates an essential obligation under the agreement, the obligation to pay restitution is limited to the damages that typically arise. Moreover, element 5 does not guarantee the Affiliate that the operation of its Web sites will be maintained without interruptions and without errors.
